steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student claims congestion worsened during the 1990s in both large cities (Miami) and smaller areas (Allentown-Bethlehem, Boise), with smaller areas less severely affected but still trending upward. The correct answer must show Miami consistently higher than a smaller area, with both rising.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — While the annual number of hours of traffic delay per commuter was always higher in the Miami, Florida, area than in the Boise, Idaho, area for each year between 1990 and 2000, the amount of traffic delay rose in both areas during this period.: Miami goes from 31 to 49 hours; Boise from 16 to 27 hours — differential severity and shared upward trend confirmed. ✓
  • Choice B — In 1992, the amount of traffic delay in the Miami, Florida, area was less than 30 hours per commuter per year.: Contradicts actual data values in the graph. ✗
  • Choice C — Even though the amount of traffic delay per commuter per year was greater in the Allentown-Bethlehem, Pennsylvania, area than in the Miami, Florida, area throughout the period from 1990 to 2000, the amounts were nearly identical in 1996.: Contradicts actual data values in the graph. ✗
  • Choice D — In at least one of the four urban areas shown, the amount of traffic delay was less than 20 hours per commuter per year at one point between 1990 and 2000.: Addresses only an incidental point rather than the sustained pattern the student describes. ✗
That is the correct answer.
